name: AGENT: StoneProfits Quote Extraction
content:
# TASK: StoneProfits Quote Extraction (SP-QUOTES-001)

Task ID: SP-QUOTES-001
Category: Data Extraction
Systems: StoneProfits
Trigger: Manual or scheduled — run weekly to keep pipeline current

## Steps

1. Navigate to StoneProfits login page
2. Log in using stored PraskForce1 credentials (credential name: StoneProfits)
3. Navigate to Presales Home > Quotes
4. For each quote in the list, extract:
   - Quote Number (e.g. Q-XXXXX)
   - Quote Date
   - Customer / Company Name
   - Contact Name
   - Project Name / Address (if present)
   - Materials listed (product names, SKUs)
   - Total quote value
   - Quote status (Draft, Sent, Accepted, Expired)
   - Salesperson (should be Brad Prasky)
5. Export data as structured JSON
6. For quotes with a property address, attempt to match against PraskForce1 pipeline properties
7. For quotes without a property address, flag for manual review

## Output Format

```json
[
  {
    "quote_number": "Q-12345",
    "date": "2026-03-15",
    "customer": "Company Name",
    "contact": "Person Name",
    "project": "123 Main St, Miami Beach",
    "materials": ["Calacatta Marble", "Travertine Pavers"],
    "total": 45000,
    "status": "Sent",
    "salesperson": "Brad Prasky"
  }
]
```

## Cross-Reference

After extraction, match quotes against:
- Pipeline properties (by address)
- Accounts (by customer/contact name)
- Permit data (by property address)

Flag matches as warm leads. Flag quotes with no pipeline match as potential new properties to track.

name: AGENT: Outlook Email Cross-Reference
content:
# TASK: Outlook Email Cross-Reference (OL-XREF-001)

Task ID: OL-XREF-001
Category: Data Extraction
Systems: Outlook
Trigger: After SP-QUOTES-001 runs — uses quote numbers as search terms

## Steps

1. Open Outlook Web (outlook.office.com)
2. Log in using stored PraskForce1 credentials (credential name: Outlook)
3. For each quote number from SP-QUOTES-001 output:
   a. Search inbox and sent for the quote number (e.g. search "Q-12345")
   b. For each matching email, extract:
      - Date sent/received
      - From / To
      - Subject line
      - Key content (first 200 chars or relevant paragraph)
      - Any mentioned timeline, decision, or next step
4. Also search for the customer name + key material terms
5. Build a communication timeline for each quote:
   - First contact date
   - Last contact date
   - Number of touchpoints
   - Last status/sentiment (positive, neutral, stale, dead)
   - Days since last contact

## Output Format

```json
[
  {
    "quote_number": "Q-12345",
    "customer": "Company Name",
    "emails": [
      {"date": "2026-03-15", "direction": "sent", "subject": "Quote for Pine Tree Dr", "snippet": "..."}
    ],
    "first_contact": "2026-03-10",
    "last_contact": "2026-03-22",
    "touchpoints": 4,
    "days_since_last": 22,
    "sentiment": "positive"
  }
]
```

## Intelligence

Combine with SP-QUOTES-001 data to identify:
- Hot leads: quote sent + recent positive email activity
- Stale leads: quote sent + no email response in 14+ days (trigger FOLLOW-UP task)
- Dead leads: quote expired + no response in 30+ days
- Active deals: quote accepted + ongoing email thread

name: AGENT: Permit Portal Scan
systems: Miami Beach Civic Access, Coral Gables EdenWeb, City of Miami iBuild, Miami-Dade County
content:
# TASK: Permit Portal Scan (SCAN-PERMITS-001)

Task ID: SCAN-PERMITS-001
Category: Lead Gen
Systems: Miami Beach Civic Access, Coral Gables EdenWeb, City of Miami iBuild, Miami-Dade County
Trigger: Daily or on-demand per property

## For Each Property in Pipeline

1. Determine which portal to check based on municipality:
   - Miami Beach → Civic Access (credential: Miami Beach Civic Access)
   - Coral Gables → EdenWeb (no login required)
   - City of Miami → iBuild (credential: City of Miami iBuild)
   - Unincorporated → Miami-Dade County portal (no login required)
   - North Miami → Call (305) 895-9830 (cannot automate)

2. Search by property address

3. For each permit found, extract:
   - Permit number
   - Permit type (demolition, new construction, remodel, etc.)
   - Status (applied, issued, in review, finaled)
   - Date filed / Date issued
   - Valuation (if listed)
   - Scope description
   - Applicant name
   - Contractor name + license
   - Architect name + license

4. Classify permit by ARCA relevance tier:
   - Tier 1 (HIGH): Demolition, new construction, major remodel ($500K+)
   - Tier 2 (MEDIUM): Interior remodel, kitchen/bath, pool/hardscape
   - Tier 3 (LOW): Windows, roofing, electrical, plumbing, fence

5. Compare against existing permits in database — flag NEW permits only

6. For new Tier 1 or Tier 2 permits:
   - Cross-reference contractor against known ARCA clients
   - Cross-reference architect against known ARCA designers
   - Update lead score
   - Trigger notification if score > threshold

name: AGENT: Sunbiz LLC Lookup
content:
# TASK: Sunbiz LLC Lookup (SCAN-SUNBIZ-001)

Task ID: SCAN-SUNBIZ-001
Category: Lead Gen
Systems: Florida Sunbiz
Trigger: When a new property enters pipeline with LLC buyer

## Steps

1. Navigate to https://search.sunbiz.org/Inquiry/CorporationSearch/ByName
2. Search for the LLC name
3. Click into the filing record
4. Extract:
   - Document Number
   - Filing Date
   - Status (Active/Inactive)
   - Principal Address
   - Registered Agent name + address
   - Manager/Member names + roles + addresses
5. For each manager/member name:
   a. Search for other LLCs where they appear (search Sunbiz by officer name)
   b. Record all associated entities
   c. Cross-reference addresses across entities (shared address = same person/group)
6. Build entity web showing all connected LLCs and properties
7. Search for the person online:
   - LinkedIn profile
   - Instagram (for designers/developers)
   - Company website
   - Likely email address (firstname@company.com pattern)
8. Update the Account in PraskForce1 with all findings

name: AGENT: PropertyReports New Sales Scan
content:
# TASK: PropertyReports New Sales Scan (SCAN-SALES-001)

Task ID: SCAN-SALES-001
Category: Lead Gen
Systems: PropertyReports.us
Trigger: Weekly scan

## Steps

1. Navigate to PropertyReports.us and log in (credential: PropertyReports.us)
2. Set filters:
   - Property type: Single Family
   - Price: $3,000,000+
   - Date: Last 7 days (or since last scan)
   - Area: Target zip codes from PraskForce1 filters
3. For each new sale:
   - Extract address, sale price, sale date, buyer name/entity
   - Check if already in PraskForce1 pipeline
   - If new: add to pipeline with status 'new'
   - If buyer is an LLC: trigger SCAN-SUNBIZ-001
   - Run property through product targeting engine (price tier + neighborhood ceiling)
4. Output list of new properties added with initial scoring

name: AGENT: Build Pipeline Intelligence
content:
# TASK: Build Pipeline Intelligence (INTEL-BUILD-001)

Task ID: INTEL-BUILD-001
Category: Intelligence
Systems: StoneProfits, Outlook, PraskForce1
Trigger: Initial setup, then monthly refresh

## Purpose
Cross-reference all data sources to build the complete intelligence layer.

## Data Flow

1. Run SP-QUOTES-001 → Extract all quotes from StoneProfits
2. Run OL-XREF-001 → Match quotes to email threads in Outlook
3. Run SCAN-PERMITS-001 → Check permits on all pipeline properties
4. Run SCAN-SUNBIZ-001 → Resolve all LLC buyers
5. Cross-reference:
   - Quotes ↔ Pipeline properties (by address)
   - Email contacts ↔ Permit contractors/architects
   - LLC principals ↔ Existing StoneProfits customers
   - Known contractors from quotes ↔ New permit filings

## Intelligence Outputs

- Lead score refinement (add CRM relationship data to scoring)
- Warm lead identification (known contact on new permit)
- Stale deal alerts (quote sent, no follow-up in 14+ days)
- New opportunity alerts (new permit on tracked property)
- Entity web expansion (new LLCs discovered via shared principals)
- Product tier confirmation (actual quote materials vs. predicted tier)

## Storage

All extracted data should be structured for Supabase storage:
- properties table: new properties from sales scan
- permits table: new permits from portal scans
- owners table: LLC resolution data from Sunbiz
- contacts table: people discovered across all sources
- lead_scores table: recalculated scores with CRM data
